db_access: blocked — politics.db SQLite version mismatch (file written by 3.50.0, drain has 3.37.2). daily_notes table could not be read.
summary: Listener appears focused on (a) Wootton High School closure and what it means for District 19 attendance areas, (b) whether any Democratic primary candidate openly opposes Maryland's sanctuary-jurisdiction direction, (c) Andrew Friedson's fiscal record specifically (asked repeatedly, not yet given a focused answer), (d) Ben Kramer's full historical voting record (not just recent sessions). The dossier-quality improvement effort is ongoing; today's Ben Kramer dossier and 4-episode series represent the new deeper baseline.
themes: Wootton closure | listener has asked directly; show has acknowledged but not resolved the policy question of why MCPS chose closure and what comes next
Sanctuary policy filter | listener wants a yes/no answer per candidate; today's Kramer ep3 delivered the cleanest one yet — Kramer has no public record opposing sanctuary
Friedson fiscal record | listener has asked repeatedly; Friedson's 专题 is queued, the answer is committed for that series
Historical voting record on incumbents | listener wants pre-2020 records, not just recent sessions; today's Kramer dossier surfaces nineteen years of record
Side-by-side primary comparison | promised for the synthesis week after all candidate series complete
open_questions: What is Wootton's status after the March 26 7-1 board vote and the CEPA April 28 administrative appeal?
Did Senator Kramer take a position on the 2025 Maryland immigration bill — yea/nay on the floor vote?
What is the outcome of the Joint Committee on Legislative Ethics review of the March 2026 Kramer/Moore comments?
How does Andrew Friedson's fiscal record on the County Council compare to other County Executive candidates?
Is there a Democratic primary opponent for any of the District 19 races that has been added since the February 24 filing deadline?
underserved_topics: Housing — Montgomery County housing supply and zoning has been mentioned but not deeply covered for any candidate
Public safety beyond DUI — gun policy, juvenile justice, MCPD oversight
Transportation — Purple Line, BRT, road safety legislation
MCPS oversight beyond Wootton — inspector general, accountability, budget
Federal-state interaction — Medicaid, Section 8, federal-grant exposure under the second Trump administration
avoid_list: The framing 'a listener asked us last week/earlier this week which Democratic primary candidates actually oppose sanctuary policy on illegal immigration' as a setup line
The Evan Glass bill requiring background screening for DHS agents joining Montgomery County police as the concrete sanctuary-policy example
The ICE agent mask-ban ordinance passed by the Montgomery County Council as the sanctuary-policy benchmark
'Two stories, properly worked through' / 'two stories, not spreading thin across six' as an episode-framing device
Governor Moore's 'Leave No One Behind' slogan contrasted with disability services cuts
The ACA marketplace quiet-downgrade story ('thousands of Marylanders have been quietly downgrading their health insurance plans')
Maryland's 90-day part-time legislature explanation ('they meet in Annapolis for ninety days, the rest of the time they're lawyers, teachers, small business owners')
The wave of 25 Maryland lawmakers retiring or running for other offices framing
The Beltsville Agricultural Research Center closure story as a District 19 candidate-record hook
Listener's own frustration about promised candidate deep-dives not delivering — now flagged across at least five episodes; do not repeat as framing
note_count: unknown — daily_notes table unreadable this run; avoid_list and themes drawn from the persistent recent-coverage memory inside the briefs the Windows scanner generated.
remediation: Upgrade drain-side SQLite to ≥3.45 OR have the Windows side write weekly_themes back into politics.db itself with the output of this JSON as input.
